title: hypericum acutum subsp. tetrapterum
intro: Hypericum acutum subsp. tetrapterum, a subspecies of the Hypericum acutum plant, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the Balkan Peninsula and Greece, also known as Four-winged St. John's Wort due to its four-winged capsule. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as hyperforin and hypericin. The plant has been reported to offer various health benefits, including wound healing, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as potential antiviral and antibacterial effects. Herbal preparations of Hypericum acutum subsp. tetrapterum can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds.
